title: sortByUpdatedAt has two contradictory signatures across spec sections
description:
The pure helper sortByUpdatedAt is declared with two incompatible signatures inside the same spec set:

1. behavioral-spec.md §1.5 line 65 declares:
   `sortByUpdatedAt(direction: 'asc' | 'desc', a: NoteRowMetadata, b: NoteRowMetadata): number — pure comparator`
   This is a 3-argument comparator that returns a comparison result.

2. behavioral-spec.md §2 pseudocode line 149 calls it as:
   `[...searchFiltered].sort(sortByUpdatedAt(sortDir, noteMetadata))`
   This shape requires a curried factory `(direction, noteMetadataMap) → (a: noteId, b: noteId) => number`, not a 3-arg comparator.

3. verification-architecture.md §1 lines 63-66 sides with the curried-factory shape: `sortByUpdatedAt(direction, noteMetadata): comparator`.

The behavioral spec therefore declares two mutually unsatisfiable signatures. An implementer cannot satisfy both REQ text (§1.5) and the canonical pseudocode (§2) at compile time; tests written against PROP-FILTER-014 will not compile against the §1.5 signature because computeVisible feeds noteIds (strings), not NoteRowMetadata records, into the sort callback.

evidence.snippet:
§1.5: sortByUpdatedAt(direction: 'asc' | 'desc', a: NoteRowMetadata, b: NoteRowMetadata): number
§2 pseudocode: [...searchFiltered].sort(sortByUpdatedAt(sortDir, noteMetadata))

remediation: Pick one shape and rewrite both occurrences. Recommended: keep the curried factory `sortByUpdatedAt(direction, noteMetadata): (aNoteId: string, bNoteId: string) => number` because computeVisible deals in noteIds, and update §1.5 to match. Then update verification-architecture.md §1 to use the same exact signature.
